source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane Other Lifting

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Subsea MEG Leak
actions_taken: BSEE onsite investigation conducted on July 24, 2018
event.summary: On July 11, 2018, at 1200 hours, an estimated 17,098 gallons of Mono Ethylene Glycol (MEG) was discharged subsea from the Na Kika Platform (MC 474-A) following repair work on PZZ 2591/92 MEG pumps. The leak occurred through leaking Automatic Operated Valve (AOV) and Shut Down Valve (SDV) into the EA2 umbilical circuit. The leak went undetected for approximately 4 days (July 11-15, 2018) due to subsea location and pollutant dissipation before reaching the surface. The incident resulted in an unauthorized discharge violation and failure to notify BSEE.
recommendations: Perform survey to determine whether any other out-of-service (OOS) lines should be positively isolated or documented
Positively isolate the EA-2 MEG line
Consider removing SDV from Sempcheck and safe charts
Include topsides interfaces for hydraulics, chemical services, and HMI in well P&A plans
Confirm no metallurgy issues for other subsea kit currently in service
Consider monitoring methods for earlier detection including real-time unallocated MEG calculation displayed on HMI
Survey existing services shared with multiple wells for passing AOVs and SDVs and ensure proper labeling and recording

hazards.name: Subsea umbilical circuits with abandoned well connections

consequences.name: Environmental pollution - MEG discharge to Gulf of Mexico

controls.name: Automatic Operated Valve (AOV)
controls.barrier_role: Isolation barrier to prevent MEG flow through abandoned well circuit
controls.lod_basis: Primary isolation device in MEG injection line

supporting_text: The Automatic Operated Valve (AOV) and Shut Down Valve (SDV) were leaking, causing MEG to flow through the valves into the EA2 umbilical circuit
The investigation revealed the leaking AOV and SDV to have caused the pollution event

controls.name: Shut Down Valve (SDV)
controls.barrier_role: Secondary isolation barrier to prevent MEG flow through abandoned well circuit
controls.lod_basis: Secondary isolation device in MEG injection line

supporting_text: The Automatic Operated Valve (AOV) and Shut Down Valve (SDV) were leaking, causing MEG to flow through the valves into the EA2 umbilical circuit
The investigation revealed the leaking AOV and SDV to have caused the pollution event

controls.name: High-pressure alarm on MEG injection line transmitter
controls.barrier_role: Detection barrier to alert operators to abnormal pressure conditions
controls.lod_basis: Alarm system for early detection of system anomalies

supporting_text: The leak to EA2 umbilical went undetected although low and high-pressure alarms were active on the transmitters
The high alarm was never reached during the time of the leak

controls.name: Low-pressure alarm on MEG injection line transmitter
controls.barrier_role: Detection barrier to alert operators to low pressure conditions
controls.lod_basis: Alarm system for early detection of system anomalies

supporting_text: The leak to EA2 umbilical went undetected although low and high-pressure alarms were active on the transmitters
The low-pressure alarm only activated when the pumps were shut down

controls.name: Operator monitoring of MEG consumption trends
controls.barrier_role: Detection barrier through manual observation of system parameters
controls.lod_basis: Routine operational monitoring to detect anomalies

supporting_text: BP production operations observed the MEG consumption was more than usual
While investigating the cause, focus was drawn to the MEG injection line at the umbilical for the MC 608 OCS-G 09838 East Anstey Well EA2

controls.name: Equipment inspection and maintenance procedures for AOV and SDV
controls.barrier_role: Preventive maintenance to ensure valve integrity and prevent seal degradation
controls.lod_basis: Preventive maintenance program to maintain barrier effectiveness

supporting_text: Equipment Failure – Inadequate Equipment Inspection: The leaking of the AOV and SDV, allowing MEG to flow through the valves into the EA2 umbilical circuit

controls.name: Positive isolation of out-of-service well connections
controls.barrier_role: Prevent flow through abandoned well circuits by positive isolation
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to eliminate pathway for unintended flow

supporting_text: The well was permanently abandoned in 2014
Positively isolate the EA-2 MEG line

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
